hero.label: MSD-RAS · University of Pennsylvania · Weitzman School of Design

aboutSplit.heading:
WHERE SYSTEMS
MEET SPACE
aboutSplit.p1: Xiao He (何骁) is a cross-disciplinary designer pursuing MSD-RAS at the University of Pennsylvania's Weitzman School of Design. With a 3.97 GPA and a foundation in architecture from China, his work bridges the physical and computational.
aboutSplit.p2: His practice explores how robotic fabrication, AI-driven knowledge systems, and spatial design can inform one another — producing work that is rigorous, replicable, and ecologically conscious.

aboutPage.label: Profile
aboutPage.heading: XIAO HE
aboutPage.tagline: Cross-disciplinary designer at the intersection of space, system, and machine.
aboutPage.p1: I build things that sit at the edge of architecture and robotics — systems that move, think, and construct. My practice spans computational design, automated fabrication, AI-driven workflows, and spatial research. I treat every project as a system problem first.
aboutPage.p2: Currently pursuing MSD-RAS at the University of Pennsylvania's Weitzman School of Design (GPA 3.97, Class of 2026). Before Penn, I trained in architecture in China, developing a foundation in structural design, modular systems, and fabrication methodology.
aboutPage.p3: My work ranges from robotic arm drawing pipelines and RAG-based AI assistants to offshore ecological architecture and modular desert housing — the common thread is rigorous system logic and a belief that design should be deployable, not just beautiful.
